our_open_deliverables.text: Share the autonomous negotiation agent (ANA) presentation and business-case white papers with healthcare client success stories
Send the PowerPoint presentation from the 6 May intro call to Jonathan
Deliver a demo covering autonomous sourcing (tail spend, medical supplies) and intake orchestration via Teams, showing flexible integration with Workday and Conga CLM.
Prepare and email Showpad package (meeting recording, customer references, product materials) to Jonathan
Include details about the no-cost value-engineering assessment and the 4-week ANA pilot (autonomous negotiations POC) in the Showpad materials

buyer_dependent_items.text: Facilitate internal meeting with VP (DTS Business Operations) and supply chain leadership to review Zycus materials and build interest for next-stage evaluation.
Provide Intermountain's spend and volume data to populate Zycus's ROI model data sheet for quantified business-case generation
Share Zycus demo materials with the relevant stakeholder team and provide feedback or proposed next steps and timeline to Zycus

competitive_position.summary: Status quo / inertia is the strongest current threat, anchored in Workday ERP recency (go-live Apr 2026, 1.5 months post-live as of 6 May call) and organizational flux (CPO turnover, supply chain reorganization). Jonathan explicitly stated on 15 May 'probably a year, two years into maybe talking about making very huge changes to that portfolio' and on 26 Jun confirmed 'no immediate architecture changes expected through 2026.' This makes deferral the default decision unless Zycus creates urgency via a compelling near-term ROI (pilot ANA for tail-spend savings, pilot intake for UX improvement) that justifies acting before Workday stabilization. Conga CLM (incumbent contract tool) is under implicit review due to Jonathan's dissatisfaction ('putting in junk data... reports not that great') but not actively threatened by a named replacement; Conga's threat level is low/dormant (not competing for same budget as Zycus, and Jonathan's pain creates an opening for Zycus CLM overlay or metadata integration). Vizient (GPO) provides spend-analysis services and is a potential competitor in the iAnalyze module but not in intake or autonomous sourcing; Vizient threat level is low/narrow (only spend analytics, and Jonathan's interest in Zycus autonomous sourcing suggests Vizient does not cover that use case). No other named competitors. Ranked by current threat: (1) Status quo / defer to 2027 = high threat (buyer's stated default), (2) Vizient spend-analytics services = low threat (narrow module overlap), (3) Conga CLM = dormant (incumbent under review, not competitive for Zycus's intake/sourcing use cases).

competitors.name: Status Quo / Inertia
competitors.quote: probably a year, two years into maybe talking about making very huge changes to that portfolio... we're too new in Workday to know if that's an improvement. We have to mature it a little bit to give it a fair value
competitors.source: Avoma 6 May and 15 May 2026 calls, Jonathan
competitors.how_we_win: Build a quantified, board-level ROI case (savings, FTE capacity gain, user-satisfaction lift) that justifies acting now rather than waiting 12-18 months. Position Zycus as a low-change-management overlay (Teams intake, flexible integration, modular adoption) that de-risks the investment and accelerates value realization during Workday stabilization period, not after it.

competitors.name: Vizient (GPO)
competitors.quote: Jonathan Kempton on 15 May 2026: 'Vizient is our GPO... they come in and both layer on services on top of some of these products... procedural analytics... firms to come in and take a look and find spend.'
competitors.source: Avoma 15 May technical discovery call, Jonathan describing Vizient's spend-analysis services
competitors.how_we_win: Differentiate Zycus iAnalyze on automation, real-time dashboards, and Workday integration (Vizient is SOW-based consulting, not a self-service platform). Position autonomous sourcing (ANA) as a capability Vizient does not provide (Vizient finds spend, Zycus automates the sourcing event and negotiation to capture it).

competitors.name: Status Quo / Inertia (Workday stabilization priority)
competitors.quote: Jonathan Kempton stated on 15 May 2026: 'we just rolled out Workday... only about a month and a half after our go live... probably a year, two years into maybe talking about making very huge changes to that portfolio.' On 26 Jun confirmed 'no immediate architecture changes expected through 2026.'
competitors.source: Avoma 15 May technical discovery call and 26 Jun demo call notes
competitors.how_we_win: Prove near-term ROI via no-cost pilots (4-week ANA pilot for tail-spend savings, intake pilot for UX improvement) that deliver measurable value before Workday stabilization is complete, creating urgency to act now rather than defer 12-18 months and risk competitive gap or normalized pain.

competitors.name: Conga CLM
competitors.quote: Jonathan Kempton on 15 May 2026: 'The Conga CLM tool... there's a high level of required fields... as they're entering data just to get to the next step... they're putting in junk... the reports end up being not that great.' Also noted 'the person who was spearheading that process is no longer with Intermountain' (Conga champion departed).
competitors.source: Avoma 15 May technical discovery call, Jonathan statements on Conga dissatisfaction
competitors.how_we_win: Position Zycus CLM as an overlay or metadata-integration partner (not a rip-and-replace) that solves the data-quality and utilization-tracking gaps Jonathan cited, or position Zycus intake/sourcing as complementary (Conga stays for contract repository, Zycus adds intake orchestration and autonomous sourcing upstream).

champion_strength.source: Avoma 6 May intro call (verbal VP-intro commitment), 15 May technical discovery (use-case shaping), 26 Jun demo (attendance and interest confirmation); SF OpportunityContactRole shows zero buyer-side roles (single-threaded); SF Next_Step__c dated 22 Jun shows 51-day gap with no VP intro executed
champion_strength.summary: Jonathan Kempton is a developing, single-threaded champion with interest and access but weak execution. He has verbally committed to facilitate VP and business-apps leadership introductions (6 May), shaped use-case discussions (15 May), and attended a demo (26 Jun), but has not delivered on multi-threading (51 days, no VP intro), ROI-model data input (51 days overdue), or follow-up meeting scheduling (no next meeting set post-demo). His advisory-only role ('not directly influencing... recommendations and advice') means he cannot drive a decision without executive sponsorship. Current engagement is active (demo 2 days ago) but passive on next steps. Risk: weak champion execution, single-threaded, no Economic Buyer access secured.
champion_strength.champion: Jonathan Kempton, Director of Vendor Performance

sources: Avoma 15 May, Jonathan: 'I'm kind of in that vendor performance management spot... we're not directly influencing other than recommendations and advice for the business apps group'
Avoma 6 May, Jonathan: 'I'll introduce you to the VP over our business apps and some of the directors there... Mike Harmar and Robin Funcannon'
Avoma 26 Jun, notes: 'Business Applications team owns strategy and will evaluate technologies; Jonathan to socialize demo with that team'
SF OpportunityContactRole query result: zero buyer-side contact roles on this opportunity
meddpicc_economic_buyer.narrative: No Economic Buyer identified or engaged in 53 days. Jonathan Kempton explicitly stated on 15 May that he is advisory-only: 'I'm kind of in that vendor performance management spot... we're not directly influencing other than recommendations and advice for the business apps group.' He named his boss as 'VP of DTS Business Operations' and identified the decision-making function as 'Business Applications team... Mike Harmar and Robin Funcannon... the business of making the decisions,' stating on 6 May 'I'll introduce you to the VP... and some of the directors there.' No such introduction has occurred. On 26 Jun Jonathan reiterated 'Business Applications team owns strategy and will evaluate technologies; Jonathan to socialize demo with that team and other stakeholder groups,' confirming he is not the budget holder. Procurement/supply-chain leadership (the new CPO successor and category-management teams) and governance/leadership are 'involved in approval during fiscal planning window' (Aug-Sep per 26 Jun call) but have not been contacted. Zero commercial discussion (no pricing, no budget confirmation, no approval-authority mapping) in any call. Gap: we do not know who controls the $200k budget, who signs contracts, or whether budget exists. The VP (DTS Biz Ops) is the closest known candidate but remains unengaged. Next move: press Jonathan for a VP intro this week (act-by 5 Jul) or escalate to the deal owner's manager (Alexa Bradley) for an executive connect to the VP and business-apps leadership, bypassing Jonathan if he cannot facilitate.

recommended_moves: Secure VP of DTS Business Operations intro and business-apps leadership meeting by 5 Jul
Deliver complete Showpad package with healthcare client references, ANA white papers, and ROI model template by 1 Jul
Secure Intermountain tail-spend volume and contract-renewal data by 5 Jul and generate conservative ROI model by 12 Jul
